reason: M.p. 164–165 °C is found in huang_2026 PDF but it belongs to compound (Sp)-4a, which is a bisphosphine OXIDE intermediate, not the final bisphosphine ligand. The row describes the compound as 'Bisphosphine ligand' which is inaccurate — 4a is the phosphine oxide precursor to the actual ligand (Sp)-5a (which has M.p. 105–106 °C).
details: PDF line 309: '(Sp)-4a. 661 mg, 87% yield; white solid; M.p. 164–165 °C'. The compound (Sp)-4a is named '...bis(2,1-phenylene)bis(diphenylphosphine oxide)' — the 'oxide' in the name indicates this is NOT the free phosphine ligand. Final bisphosphine ligand (Sp)-5a has M.p. 105–106 °C. The mp value 164–165 and quote are present in the paper but the compound type description is incorrect.
